name: Shabib ibn Abdullah
title: Lion of the Movement
shortDescription: One of the brave warriors of Kufa who drew his sword during the first attack of Umar Sa'd's army and was martyred valiantly.
fullStory:
Shabib ibn Abdullah al-Nahshali (or Shabib ibn Harith) was a renowned warrior and sincere Shia of Kufa. He was among those whose hearts were tied with the love of the Ahl al-Bayt, and when he learned of Imam Hussain's (AS) presence in Karbala, he joined his caravan.

On the morning of Ashura, when Umar Sa'd's 30,000-strong army, under his command, fired a rain of arrows towards the tents of Imam Hussain's (AS) few companions, Shabib was one of those who immediately drew his sword.

Instead of taking cover against this brutal assault, he stood shoulder to shoulder with other warriors like Habib and Zuhayr, made his chest a shield, and charged into the heart of the enemy to defend the sanctuary of the Ahl al-Bayt.

In this mass attack (the first attack), which was a very fierce and unequal battle, Shabib fought bravely. Despite the enemy's numerical superiority, he brought down a number of them with his combat skills.

Finally, after receiving numerous injuries in this initial attack, this Kufan warrior fell to the ground and attained the great grace of martyrdom, recording his name among the early martyrs of Ashura.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى شَبيبِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ النَّهْشَلِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Shabib ibn Abdullah al-Nahshali.
